seoTitle: Real day rate: full-time vs fractional mandate | Quebec
seoDescription: In Quebec, fractional consulting is often misread as part-time employment or a one-off gig. Compare a loaded full-time seat with a results-first day rate (senior dev or PM).
title: Real day rate: full-time vs fractional mandate
lede: I still get put in the wrong box too often. Here is what it really costs once a full-time seat is loaded against my day rate.

posture.title: The real difference is the posture
paragraphs: Not a part-time employee. Not a consultant for a one-off mandate. The agreement is structured over 3 months, 6 months, sometimes a year: we define expected outcomes together, and goals evolve with what has been delivered.
My job is not to keep the wheel spinning. It is to spot what blocks progress, name what slows execution, and take the means to move things forward, even when that challenges some habits.
The level of engagement is not the same. I insist on results. If the status quo takes over, or if the necessary decisions never get made, the conditions to deliver are gone: I exit myself.

mandate.title: How the mandate works
mandate.lede: In practice, here is how operations work on my side.
items: I remain responsible for my schedule. We allocate worked days to keep management simple, but the hours stay under my control: I can change them, adjust available days, or withdraw days (with compensation when needed).
I cover my own tools, computer equipment, conferences, vacations and time off.
We frame the outcome and the technical scope together; how I execute stays mine (methods, tools, work organization).

If factors outside my control block delivery of the results negotiated up front, despite my attempts to address them, I end the mandate under the exit clause. In practice, I am almost always the one who ends it for that reason. An employee does not do that.

help.developer: Senior dev in a tech SME: CNESST IT services (0.79%), lighter internal coordination, fractional mandate ideally 1-2 d/wk (max 3).
help.productManager: Product manager: same office-IT CNESST rate, more internal alignment, longer ramp-up, fractional mandate ideally 1-2 d/wk (max 3).

employeeCost.title: Payroll loads and productive days
employeeCost.lede: Gross salary, payroll taxes, benefits and productive days.

companyPayroll.label: Company total payroll
companyPayroll.help: Determines the HSF (health services fund) rate. SME at $1M or less: 1.65%. Above that, the rate scales up to 4.26%.

benefits.label: Employer benefits
benefits.help: Group insurance, group RRSP, other attributable benefits. Typical in tech: 5 to 8% of gross.

bonus.label: Bonus / variable pay
bonus.help: Annual bonus, often 10 to 15% of base for a senior profile. Added to pensionable pay (payroll taxes applied). Estimated bonus: {{amount}}.

productiveDays.label: Productive days per year (steady state)
productiveDays.help: After year 1: 260 workdays minus holidays (~10), vacation (~20), sick/personal (~6), meetings and non-productive time (~9). Initial ramp-up is modeled separately.

recruitment.label: Recruitment cost (one-time, year 1)
recruitment.help: Posting, agency, manager interview time, HR tools. Typical senior range: 10 to 20% of gross. Counted once, in year 1.

onboardingMonths.label: Ramp-up duration
onboardingMonths.help: Period paid at full salary before full productivity. Senior profile: often 2 to 4 months.

onboardingProductivity.label: Productivity during ramp-up
onboardingProductivity.help: 60%: employee is paid 100% but delivers about 60% of a productive day. Ramp-up reduces delivered days, not the salary paid.

autonomy.title: Coordination, tools and workplace
autonomy.lede: An employee needs supervision, alignment and HR follow-up. A senior fractional consultant is self-directed: prioritization, delivery, minimal escalation.

coordination.label: Internal coordination hours per week
coordination.help: 1:1s, planning, reviews, HR follow-up. Senior employee: often 2 to 4 h/wk. Autonomous consultant: close to 0.

help.developer: Amortized laptop (~3 yr), IDE, GitHub, dev licenses, monitor, headset. Typical SME: $4,000 to $7,000/yr.
help.productManager: Laptop, Figma, analytics, Miro, product SaaS licenses. Typical SME: $5,000 to $8,000/yr.

consultant.help: Consultant side: computer, software and infra are included in the day rate ($0 extra for you).

workplace.label: Workplace cost (annual, per seat)
workplace.help: Average cost allocated per seat: rent, fit-out, amortized furniture and operating costs at the office; allowance, home equipment and internet when remote. Montreal office: ~$5,000 to $8,000/yr; hybrid: ~$3,000 to $4,000; remote: ~$1,000 to $1,500. If your office is already paid for, the marginal cost of one more seat can be lower.
workplace.consultantHelp: Consultant side: workspace, hardware and connection are included in the day rate ($0 for you, office or remote).

lifecycle.title: Turnover and end of employment
lifecycle.lede: An employee eventually leaves: you re-hire and re-train, and a departure can trigger notice pay. A consultant mandate simply ends at term, with no severance.

tenure.label: Average tenure
tenure.help: Average time in seat before a departure. In tech, often 2 to 4 years. Shorter tenure means recruitment and ramp-up recur more often.

severance.label: Notice / severance
severance.help: Statutory notice (Quebec LNT): 1 to 8 weeks by tenure. Wrongful-dismissal (common law) risk can go higher. Amortized over average tenure.

dayRate.label: My day rate
dayRate.note: Standard rates. Rescue or high-risk: quoted case by case.

billedDays.label: Worked days / week
billedDays.help: I take on 3 d/wk maximum. The fractional sweet spot: 1 to 2 d/wk, where senior expertise costs the least and delivers the most. At 4 d/wk or more, hiring is more advantageous (and I do not take those mandates).

ideal.headline: At {{days}} d/wk: {{amount}} saved per year ({{pct}}).
ideal.sub: This is where the model delivers most: senior, results-oriented expertise, available right away, with no fixed commitment.

ceiling.headline: At {{days}} d/wk (max capacity): still {{amount}} saved per year ({{pct}}).
ceiling.sub: We are nearing the point where a full-time seat holds up. I do not take mandates of 4 d/wk or more: beyond that, fractional loses its edge.

hire.headline: In this scenario, cost leans toward the employee.
hire.sub: At this salary and rate, hiring is cheaper over the year. Fractional still fits for focused, results-oriented senior expertise with no commitment.

verdict.cap: Availability: 1 to 3 d/wk (max 3). The sweet spot stays 1 to 2 d/wk. It is the results-oriented mindset that makes the difference, not the number of hours.

points.flex: No payroll taxes or fixed commitment

dayCost.note: Per day, a senior rate can exceed a loaded employee's cost: that is the price of expertise available right away, with no recruitment or ramp-up. The advantage shows up on the annual commitment, which is lower because you only pay for useful days.

breakdown.title: Quebec {{year}} payroll tax breakdown
breakdown.footnote: HSF at {{fss}}% (SME payroll) · CNESST IT / office services at 0.79%. QPP, EI and QPIP contributions are capped: on high salaries, the effective payroll load drops.

recurring.title: Recurring costs around the seat
recurring.autonomyFootnote: {{hours}} h/wk of internal coordination, valued at {{hourly}}/h (manager, team, HR time).

lifecycle.title: Turnover and severance (amortized per year)
lifecycle.footnote: Amortized over a {{tenure}}-year tenure. Recurring ramp-up removes ~{{lostDays}} productive days per year on average.

yearOne.title: First-year costs (one-time)
yearOne.rampNote: Ramp-up: {{months}} months at {{productivity}}% productivity, i.e. -{{lostDays}} productive days in year 1. The salary itself is paid at 100%.

annual.employeeSub: loaded salary, tools and coordination, paid all year
annual.consultantSub: Continuous 12-month mandate: {{vacation}} vacation weeks, ~{{workingWeeks}} billable weeks (holidays pro-rated).
annual.savingConsultant: At this cadence, the fractional mandate costs {{amount}} less per year ({{pct}}), and you only pay for useful days.
annual.savingEmployee: At this cadence, the employee costs {{amount}} less per year ({{pct}}): if you need 5 d/wk continuously over several years, hiring makes sense.

autonomy.text: On a fractional mandate, a senior consultant prioritizes, delivers and communicates without an internal supervision net. Computer, software, infra and workspace are included in the day rate: you do not fund them on top. You pay for output, not the organization around the seat.

taxes.note: Note: the consultant charges GST/QST (14.975%), but these are recoverable (input tax credits) for a registered business. So it is not a real cost, only a cash-flow item.

consultantConsiderations.text: To stay honest: when the consultant leaves, some knowledge can leave with them (document it), their availability is shared across a few clients, and a little context ramp-up is still needed at the start. In return, no fixed commitment, no payroll taxes, and senior expertise available right away.

summary.text: In steady state, a {{salary}} profile costs about {{employeeSteadyDay}} per productive day once loaded and amortized. A day rate of {{consultantDay}} may look higher per day, but it is senior expertise available from week 1, with no recruitment, ramp-up or supervision. Above all, the employee commits {{employeeAnnual}} per year full time, while the fractional mandate at {{billedDays}} d/wk comes to {{consultantAnnual}} per year, with no fixed cost: you pay for useful days, not the seat.

disclaimer: Estimates for comparison only, based on 2026 Quebec payroll tax rates (Revenu Québec, RCGT). Ramp-up reduces productive days (salary is still paid at 100%) and recruitment is counted once, in year 1. Internal coordination is valued at the employee's loaded hourly cost. On the consultant side, we annualize a continuous 12-month mandate: {{vacation}} vacation weeks, i.e. {{workingWeeks}} working weeks, minus Quebec's {{holidays}} statutory holidays pro-rated to cadence (a holiday may not fall on a client day). At 3 d/wk, a 10% volume discount applies to the day rate. Adjust for your reality and validate with your accountant, HR team or WebRAS for a precise case.
ctaLead: If this posture sounds like what you are looking for, and the numbers speak to your context, we can talk for 30 minutes.
ctaTeaser: We will clarify expected outcomes and the cadence that makes sense.
